title: Pay Your Credit Card on the Wrong Day, Lose $21K
hook: Almost everyone pays their credit card on the due date — but that's the worst possible day to pay if you want a high credit score.
angle: The statement close date vs. due date distinction is virtually unknown but can swing your credit score 50 points and cost $21K on a 30-year mortgage.
audience: Anyone with a credit card planning a mortgage, car loan, or who wants better interest rates
key_numbers: 50-point swing between optimal and danger zones
$21,000 extra mortgage interest from a low score
Bureaus snapshot your balance on statement close date — not the due date
